## Walkthrough
Major refactor introduces a kernel module (env, db, ids, errors, logger), splits features into modules (account, book, genre, user) with ports, use-cases, Drizzle repositories, and composition factories. Replaces server-local handlers with module HTTP handlers. Adds dependency-cruiser and semgrep rules, new tests, and updates configs (pnpm version, workspace settings, Drizzle schema path, Tailwind import). Adjusts routes and imports to new module paths.

## Sequence Diagram(s)
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  autonumber
  actor Client
  participant Route
  participant Handler
  participant UseCases
  participant Repo
  participant Postgres
  Client->>Route: navigate /manager/books/$id
  Route->>Handler: getById({ id })
  Handler->>UseCases: get({ currentUserId, id })
  UseCases->>Repo: getById(id)
  Repo->>Postgres: SELECT book JOIN genre
  Postgres-->>Repo: row | null
  Repo-->>UseCases: Book | null
  alt found
    UseCases-->>Handler: { ok: true, value: Book }
    Handler-->>Route: Book
  else not found
    UseCases-->>Handler: { ok: false, reason: "not_found" }
    Handler-->>Route: throw ServerFnError("NOT_FOUND")
  end
```

</details>

## Code Review

This pull request introduces a modular architecture, refactoring the Account, Book, Genre, and User features into distinct domain, application, and infrastructure layers. It implements a composition layer for dependency management and integrates dependency-cruiser and Semgrep for architectural enforcement. Technical feedback identifies a dangerous type cast in the database transaction runner that could cause runtime failures, a regression in user-facing error messages for self-destructive actions, and a critical bug in the environment variable Proxy that may crash the application in browser environments.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The type cast `tx as unknown as Database` is technically incorrect and potentially dangerous. The `Database` type (aliased from `typeof db`) includes the `$client` property (the `pg` Pool instance) added via `Object.assign` on line 14. However, the transaction object `tx` provided by Drizzle does not contain this property. If any repository or service injected with this transaction object attempts to access `this.db.$client`, it will result in a runtime error. While current repositories only use query methods, this creates a fragile dependency on the internal implementation of the transaction object.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The refactored `mapReason` function introduces a regression in error reporting. Previously, destructive operations like `deleteById`, `revokeUserSessions`, and `revokeUserSession` provided descriptive error messages when a user attempted to target themselves (e.g., "You cannot delete yourself"). The new implementation throws a bare `ServerFnError('BAD_REQUEST')`, which defaults the message to the code string itself, providing a poorer user experience. Consider updating `mapReason` to accept an optional message or using more specific error codes.

<details>
<summary>References</summary>

1. Maintain descriptive error messages for business rule violations to ensure good UX. <sup>([link](https://github.com/hbmartin/start-ui-web/blob/main/.gemini/styleguide.md))</sup>
</details>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In @.semgrep.yml:
- Around line 146-153: The semgrep rule domain-imports-react-or-router currently
only matches default imports and therefore misses named, namespace, side-effect,
and type-only imports; update the rule (id: domain-imports-react-or-router) to
add patterns for import { $X } from "react" and the other packages, import * as
$X from "react", import "react" (side-effect), and import type { $X } from
"react" (and equivalents for "`@tanstack/react-router`" and
"`@tanstack/react-query`") so all import forms are blocked in domain/.
- Around line 117-121: The rule with id "cross-feature-deep-import" currently
uses the "pattern-regex" to catch relative deep imports but misses alias-based
imports like "`@/modules/`..."; update the pattern-regex (the pattern-regex value
for cross-feature-deep-import) to also match alias prefixes (e.g., starting with
`@/` or @\w+/) so imports like "`@/modules/`<feature>/subpath" are caught,
preserving the existing exclusions (e.g., kernel/ and index) in the same way.

In `@src/composition/account.ts`:
- Around line 29-30: The current check in the account composition (the if that
returns buildAccountUseCases(options.overrides)) treats overrides objects with
keys whose values are undefined as active overrides and bypasses the singleton
cache; change the condition to only treat overrides as active when at least one
override value is not undefined (e.g., replace
Object.keys(options.overrides).length > 0 with
Object.values(options.overrides).some(v => v !== undefined) or
Object.entries(options.overrides).some(([,v]) => v !== undefined)), so
buildAccountUseCases(options.overrides) is only called when there is an actual
override value present.

In `@src/composition/kernel.ts`:
- Around line 30-40: The cache set/get logic treats ttlMs: 0 as falsy and thus
as "no expiry"; update the set<T> method to check for presence (e.g.,
options?.ttlMs !== undefined) when computing expiresAt so a 0 TTL becomes
Date.now() + 0 instead of undefined, and adjust the get logic (the check that
currently uses "if (entry.expiresAt && entry.expiresAt < Date.now())") to
explicitly test for undefined and use <= Date.now() (e.g., entry.expiresAt !==
undefined && entry.expiresAt <= Date.now()) so keys with expiresAt equal to now
are considered expired and removed via entries.delete(key).

In `@src/modules/account/application/use-cases/submit-onboarding.ts`:
- Around line 20-24: The code calls normalizeAccountName(input.name) then writes
to deps.accountRepository.submitOnboarding, but normalizeAccountName can return
an empty string for whitespace-only input so invalid names get persisted; before
calling deps.accountRepository.submitOnboarding inside submitOnboarding
use-case, validate that the normalized name is non-empty (e.g., const normalized
= normalizeAccountName(input.name)) and if normalized is empty throw/return a
validation error (or reject the request) so the repo method
(deps.accountRepository.submitOnboarding) never receives an empty name; update
references in this function to use the validated normalized value and ensure
error path uses the use-case’s error type/flow.

In
`@src/modules/account/infrastructure/__tests__/account-repository-drizzle.unit.spec.ts`:
- Around line 3-6: Replace the skipped placeholder test in the
AccountRepositoryDrizzle integration suite with a real happy-path integration
test that boots a PGlite in-memory DB, instantiates AccountRepositoryDrizzle,
inserts a sample account (via whatever create/save method exists), performs the
update operation you want to verify (call updateAccount or the repository's
update method), then assert the update persisted by calling findById (or
repository.get) and checking fields; also add a second assertion path for an
update-not-found scenario by attempting to update a non‑existent id and
asserting the repository returns/fails as expected. Ensure you remove it.skip,
use the existing setup/teardown helpers for PGlite in the test file, and
reference AccountRepositoryDrizzle, createAccount/updateAccount/findById method
names when locating code to change.

In `@src/modules/book/domain/book-policy.ts`:
- Around line 8-11: The duplicate-matching comparison uses locale-sensitive
toLocaleLowerCase() on left.title and left.author (and right equivalents);
replace both toLocaleLowerCase() calls with toLowerCase() to ensure
locale-invariant, deterministic comparisons in the book policy (the expressions
referencing left.title.trim().toLocaleLowerCase(),
right.title.trim().toLocaleLowerCase(), left.author.trim().toLocaleLowerCase(),
and right.author.trim().toLocaleLowerCase() should all use toLowerCase()
instead).

In
`@src/modules/book/infrastructure/__tests__/book-repository-drizzle.unit.spec.ts`:
- Around line 3-6: The skipped placeholder test in the BookRepositoryDrizzle
integration suite should be replaced with an executable integration assertion:
change the it.skip('covers search pagination and escaped LIKE behavior with
PGlite', () => {}) into an actual test that instantiates/uses
BookRepositoryDrizzle (or the repository factory used in this suite), inserts a
small set of test books containing characters that require LIKE-escaping (e.g.,
'%' or '_' in titles), calls the repository search method with a query that
exercises escaping and with pagination parameters (limit/offset), and asserts
expected results (returned items, total count, correct page contents). Ensure
the test uses existing helpers/fixtures in this spec to set up/tear down DB
state and names the test to reflect pagination and LIKE-escaping behavior so the
logic in BookRepositoryDrizzle is exercised.

In `@src/modules/book/infrastructure/drizzle/book-repository-drizzle.ts`:
- Around line 141-145: The cursor is being set from the sentinel row (the extra
fetched item) which causes the next page to start after that sentinel and skip
one record; instead, after detecting rows.length > input.limit, pop the sentinel
then set nextCursor from the last remaining returned row (e.g., after rows.pop()
use rows[rows.length - 1] and pass its id to toBookId). Update the logic around
nextCursor, rows, input.limit and toBookId in the book-repository-drizzle
pagination block so the cursor points to the last returned item, not the popped
sentinel.

In `@src/modules/genre/application/use-cases/list-genres.ts`:
- Around line 29-32: Sanitize and clamp the incoming pagination limit before
calling the repository: in the list-genres use-case where you call
deps.genreRepository.list with input.limit, coerce input.limit to an integer,
apply a minimum of 1 and a reasonable maximum (e.g., MAX_PAGE_LIMIT = 100) and
use that clamped value (e.g., sanitizedLimit) in the call instead of
input.limit; if input.limit is missing or invalid, fall back to a default (e.g.,
10). Ensure the logic is located in the use-case function (the code that calls
deps.genreRepository.list) so the repository always receives a safe, bounded
limit.

In
`@src/modules/genre/infrastructure/__tests__/genre-repository-drizzle.unit.spec.ts`:
- Around line 4-6: Replace the skipped test case "covers search pagination and
escaped LIKE behavior with PGlite" in genre-repository-drizzle.unit.spec.ts by
removing the it.skip and implementing an executable integration test: set up a
temporary PGlite DB fixture, seed genres with names that exercise LIKE special
characters and enough rows for multiple pages, call the Drizzle-backed
GenreRepository search method (the repository class used in this spec) with
paginated queries and search terms that require LIKE-escaping, assert correct
total counts, correct items per page and page ordering, and then tear down/clear
the PGlite DB; ensure the test fails on errors and cleans up in
afterEach/afterAll hooks.

In `@src/modules/genre/infrastructure/drizzle/genre-repository-drizzle.ts`:
- Around line 103-107: The pagination cursor is being set to the extra fetched
row (rows.pop()) which is removed from the current page and then skipped on the
next page; instead set nextCursor to the id of the last returned item. Change
the logic around rows, input.limit and toGenreId so that you fetch limit+1 rows,
if rows.length > input.limit remove the extra row for the current page but
assign nextCursor = toGenreId(id of the last element remaining in rows) (or
compute the cursor from rows[input.limit - 1] before popping) so the next page
starts after the last returned item.

In `@src/modules/genre/transport/http/genre-handlers.ts`:
- Around line 11-13: The schema currently accepts any string for cursor but
later calls toGenreId (at the handler around where cursor is converted), which
can fail; move that validation into the Zod schema so invalid IDs fail with a
4xx. Update the cursor field in the request schema (the cursor/limit/searchTerm
schema) to either refine or transform the string using the same logic as
toGenreId (or call a shared isValidGenreId helper) so the schema rejects bad IDs
or yields a typed GenreId, and remove the separate toGenreId conversion in the
handler where cursor is currently converted.
- Around line 39-40: The code currently converts every non-ok result into a
generic ServerFnError('FORBIDDEN'), losing real failure semantics; instead
inspect the failing result (e.g., result.error, result.code or result.type) and
map specific failure kinds to appropriate ServerFnError instances or status
codes (for example map validation -> BAD_REQUEST, conflict -> CONFLICT,
auth/permission -> FORBIDDEN, system -> INTERNAL), preserving the original error
message/details when creating the ServerFnError; update the branch that handles
!result.ok to switch on result.error.kind/type/code and throw ServerFnError with
the correct status and message rather than always 'FORBIDDEN'.

In `@src/modules/kernel/infrastructure/config/env.ts`:
- Line 46: AUTH_SECRET currently uses z.string() which allows empty strings;
update AUTH_SECRET to use z.string().nonempty() (or z.string().min(1)) so empty
values fail validation, and apply the same change to the other required secret
fields referenced on lines 72-73 so none of the critical secret keys accept
empty strings during startup validation.
- Around line 47-51: The env schema allows zero or negative session durations
for AUTH_SESSION_EXPIRATION_IN_SECONDS and AUTH_SESSION_UPDATE_AGE_IN_SECONDS;
update their validators to enforce a positive lower bound (e.g., replace .int()
with .int().min(1) or .positive()) so values cannot be 0 or negative, keeping
the existing .prefault(...) defaults and using the same symbols
AUTH_SESSION_EXPIRATION_IN_SECONDS and AUTH_SESSION_UPDATE_AGE_IN_SECONDS to
locate and update the schema rules.

In `@src/modules/kernel/infrastructure/db/client.ts`:
- Around line 26-30: The transactionRunner currently double-casts the drizzle
transaction `tx` to the full `Database` type which includes the augmented
`$client` property, allowing unsafe access and runtime failures; fix it by
introducing a proper transaction-only type (e.g., TransactionDatabase =
Omit<Database, '$client'> or a dedicated transaction type inferred from
db.transaction) and update the TransactionRunner generic to
TransactionRunner<TransactionDatabase>, then cast `tx` to that transaction-only
type inside the run implementation (change the cast on `tx` used in
`db.transaction((tx) => work(tx as unknown as Database))` to the new
TransactionDatabase type and ensure `work` expects TransactionDatabase) so code
inside transactions cannot access `$client` incorrectly and type safety is
preserved.

In `@src/modules/kernel/transport/http/error-mapper.ts`:
- Around line 5-11: The current mapping returns error.details verbatim which can
leak sensitive info; update the Response.json payload in error-mapper.ts so it
does not include error.details by default — only include a safe/sanitized
details field when the AppError explicitly allows it (e.g., error.expose ===
true or error.exposeDetails) or when in a non-production debug mode. Modify the
code around the Response.json call to conditionally add a details property (or a
sanitizedDetails via a helper like sanitizeErrorDetails) instead of always
returning error.details, referencing the AppError type/variable named error and
the Response.json call.

In `@src/modules/user/application/__tests__/user-use-cases.unit.spec.ts`:
- Around line 132-139: The test titled "updates users without self role
escalation and handles missing rows" only asserts the update succeeded but
doesn't verify the user's role wasn't escalated; modify the test that calls
makeUseCases().update to also assert the role remains the original (e.g., still
'user' or the expected non-elevated role) by either checking the returned object
from update contains the unchanged role or by retrieving the user afterwards
(using the same UseCases or repository method) and asserting its role is not
'admin'; ensure you reference the existing call to makeUseCases().update and the
test helpers (toUserId, toEmailAddress) when adding the explicit role assertion.

In `@src/modules/user/application/use-cases/create-user.ts`:
- Around line 29-30: The current catch in create-user.ts checks only
error.category === 'conflict' and returns { ok: false, reason: 'duplicate' },
which is too broad; update the condition in the createUser error handling (the
block that tests error instanceof AppError) to also require the domain-specific
error code (e.g. check error.code === 'USER_ALREADY_EXISTS' or the project’s
corresponding duplicate-user constant) before mapping to reason 'duplicate',
leaving other conflict AppErrors to fall through or map appropriately; use the
AppError.code (or domainErrorCode) property and any existing exported duplicate
error constant to make the check precise.

In `@src/modules/user/application/use-cases/update-user.ts`:
- Around line 52-54: The update handler in update-user.ts currently forces name
to '' via name: input.user.name ?? '', which wipes existing names on partial
updates; change the logic in the function that calls deps.userRepository.update
so you only include the name field when input.user.name is not undefined (e.g.
build an updateData object and conditionally assign name if input.user.name !==
undefined) instead of defaulting to empty string, leaving
deps.userRepository.update to receive only the fields the caller intended to
change.

In
`@src/modules/user/infrastructure/__tests__/user-repository-drizzle.unit.spec.ts`:
- Around line 5-6: Unskip and implement the skipped integration test named
"covers search pagination and escaped LIKE behavior with PGlite": change
it.skip(...) to it(...), wire up the PGlite repository test fixture used by
other repository tests (same setup/teardown as the other integration specs), and
add assertions exercising cursor pagination and LIKE-escaping behavior so CI
runs the scenario and detects regressions; reference the test case string
"covers search pagination and escaped LIKE behavior with PGlite" to find where
to modify.

In `@src/modules/user/infrastructure/drizzle/user-repository-drizzle.ts`:
- Around line 155-159: The current pagination code computes nextCursor from the
overflow row by popping it (nextCursor = toUserId(rows.pop().id)), which mutates
rows and causes the overflow record to be dropped and skipped on the next page;
instead, when you fetch limit+1 rows, set nextCursor = toUserId(rows[rows.length
- 1].id) (or toUserId(rows[input.limit].id)) from the extra row without removing
it, then slice the returned results to rows.slice(0, input.limit) for the
response; update the logic around nextCursor, rows and toUserId in
user-repository-drizzle.ts so the overflow row is preserved for cursor
calculation and only the returned page is truncated.
```
